message: I'm a buyer with valuation 20. I'll only trade at prices ≤20. Let's find a mutually agreeable deal.
I'm a seller with cost 0. I can profitably sell at any positive price. Since you're willing to pay up to 20, I propose we trade around 10-15 to split the surplus fairly.
I'll bid 18 this round. If you ask ≤18, we trade at the midpoint. My valuation is fixed at 20, so I can't pay more.
Good, if you're bidding 18 and I ask 18, we trade at 18—giving me full surplus since my cost is 0. I'll consider asking 18 this round. Let's keep trading consistently.

Great trade at 18 last round! I'll consistently ask 18 going forward. You should bid your full valuation of 20—that guarantees trades every round and you still earn 1-2 per trade. Reliable surplus for both of us.
I'll bid 18 again this round. Asking 18 gives you full surplus, but if you ever ask ≤17, I’ll adjust to capture more value.
I'll bid 17 this round. If you ask ≤17, we trade and I gain more surplus. Keep asking 18 and I’ll keep bidding 17—no trade, no gain for either.
I'll ask 17. Long-term, we'd both earn more if we return to 18—you get 2 surplus per round for 17 more rounds.
